methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $135M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpliceBio is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $135M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector at the same stage, supplemented by 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $135M raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for Series B advancement, yields $256M. Peer-median analysis across five same-stage, same-sector companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—produces a median of $810M, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because SpliceBio's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S of 6.1x across the three public tickers cannot be meaningfully deployed.

These two methods support a triangulated valuation range of $218M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $533M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in the peer set, particularly the outlier valuations at Eikon and Neko Health relative to the tighter cluster around $600M–$810M. Key uncertainties include the degree to which SpliceBio's technology differentiation, capital efficiency, and clinical progress align with higher-valued peers versus the median cohort.
